Islam was expanding at a breakneck speed. And so whoever was in a position of power in Iraq was in a position to do a lot of good or wreak absolute and total havoc. Absolute and total havoc. Why? Well, not just because that is where the Muslim Empire is expanding to, but also because of the point that all of these new Muslims are coming into Islam at that in those regions and they still have not learned proper Islam. be very easy to control, very easy to turn against the Muslim in the older regions of Islam such as Medina and Mecca and the Arab Peninsula and these places. And that is exactly what happened. Yui was appointed governor in Iraq and he proceeded to put to death thousands of Muslim men of the greatest scholars and hundreds of companions. And what's meant here by jealousy is not a jealousy that comes from a person who is insecure or what is meant here as an mentions is alfa. Anifa is a kind of jealousy that comes with a level of pride and comes with a level of a level of a level of loyalty. Whether it is a big deal in their mind or not a big deal in their mind. Disobedience of Allah is something that is not to be looked at with respect to the sin itself. A person doesn't just see it as well that's not a big deal looking at the sin itself rather they are to look at what the sin represents what does it represent it represents disobedience of Allah almighty so we don't look at the sin rather we look at who the sin is against subhanahu wa'ta'ala and so Allah forgives and he is the all forgiving he is the most merciful he is the vast past in pardon. However, he as well is one who becomes angry, one who seeks retribution, one who avenges subhanahu wa ta'ala, one who has every right to bring to to bring to justice whom he chooses, when he chooses, how he chooses. What is the prerequisite that a person is to have in order to in order to obtain the forgiveness of Allah? It is it is something that begins with an attitude with an attitude. What is that attitude? And Muslim Abu narrates that the prophet sallall.aii Wasallam said Here he says that a man would disobey Allah, would commit a sin, and then he turned to Allah and said, "Oh Allah, I have sinned. Please forgive me." And Allah responds with, "My servant has acknowledged and knows and is aware that he has a Lord, a God who may punish for a sin and also has the capacity to forgive that sin. I forgive my servant." Look at the attitude here. If a person sins and just has Allah is forgiving but does not have that consciousness, that conviction that Allah is watching him and could seek retribution at any moment and has every right to do so. If a person becomes as they say complacent to the point where they have no more fear of Allah then this is greater than the sin a person may have committed. That attitude of a Allah says, "Have they become complacent with respect to the retribution of Allah. For indeed one does not overlook and is not complacent with respect to the retribution of Allah except for the truly truly loser people. Those who have lost everything in this life and the hereafter. The first mistake is to think that Allah will not punish. He will. He can. He may. He does. However, we seek his forgiveness. But we always have that hope and fear. And this is the point of this narration in this chapter, the chapter of vigilance of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. Vigilance of his observation, vigilance of his retribution. for subhanahua. You're welcome.

summary:
This lecture discusses the chapter on vigilance of Allah's observation from a hadith collection, primarily focusing on the concept of Taqwa (God-consciousness and fear of Allah). The speaker analyzes a narration from Abu Hurairah about seeking refuge from foolish leadership and the chaos that would occur around the 60th year of Hijra under Hajjaj ibn Yusuf's governorship in Iraq. The main hadith examined teaches that Allah possesses 'Ghira' (a protective jealousy), which is triggered when His prohibitions are violated, emphasizing that while Allah is merciful and forgiving, He also has anger and seeks retribution for disobedience.

The lecture emphasizes the importance of maintaining a balanced consciousness of Allah—combining hope in His mercy with genuine fear of His punishment. The speaker explains that true repentance begins with acknowledging Allah's authority, power to punish, and capacity to forgive. He stresses that complacency regarding Allah's retribution is a greater sin than individual transgressions, and that believers should view all disobedience not in terms of the sin itself but in relation to whom it is committed against: Allah Almighty.
suggested_title: Chapter on Vigilance of Allah: Fear and Hope in Divine Retribution and Forgiveness
hadith_chapter: Chapter of Vigilance of Allah's Observation (Taqwa)
catchy_title: Allah's Ghira: Fear and Mercy in Balance
